frameworks.name: agentic multi-agent LLM systems
frameworks.one_line: Swarms of collaborating LLM agents as an influence-warfare architecture.

frameworks.name: computational propaganda / information warfare
frameworks.one_line: AI-enabled influence operations targeting democratic discourse at scale.

frameworks.name: chain-of-thought prompting
frameworks.one_line: Reasoning-enhancement technique repurposed to fabricate more convincing falsehoods.

core_question: How can swarms of malicious agentic AI systems threaten democratic processes, and where should governance intervene?
key_assumptions: LLM-generated content can match or exceed human-written persuasiveness
Multi-agent coordination scales influence campaigns without losing credibility
Harms originate from design choices, commercial incentives, and weak governance
key_findings: Generative tools expand propaganda output while preserving credibility
AI-written falsehoods are rated as more human-like than human-written ones
Chain-of-thought prompting also amplifies persuasiveness of false content
Agent swarms can autonomously infiltrate communities and fabricate consensus
interpretations: Fusion of LLM reasoning with multi-agent architectures opens a new frontier of information warfare
Mitigation requires interventions across design, commercial, and governance leverage points
